subject: [RFC PATCH 20/40] mm: page_alloc: prefer reclaim over tainting clean superpageblocks

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory allocation algorithm to prefer reclaiming pages over tainting clean superpageblocks when allocating memory. The change aims to prevent unnecessary tainting of superpageblocks, which can lead to fragmentation and decreased performance. To achieve this, the patch reorders the allocation fallback cascade to try reclaim and compaction before tainting clean superpageblocks. It also preserves the ALLOC_NOFRAGMENT flag through the slowpath and drops it as a last resort when OOM is reached. Testing shows that this change maintains unmovable pageblocks at zero throughout heavy workloads, where previously 2-3 additional SPBs would become tainted.
